div.head: 477. Memorandum From the President’s Special Assistant for National Security Affairs (Bundy) to President JohnsonSource: Johnson Library, National Security File, Memos to the President, McGeorge Bundy, Vol. 15. No classification marking.

list.head: RE
list.item: Southern Rhodesia and the United Nations

div.p: We hope that we can get friends to sponsor our moderate resolution, but it is a possibility that we would have to put it forward ourselves. I think you will want to give Goldberg tactical flexibility on this, but if you have a strong view, I know he would want to hear it. He will, of course, act under Dean Rusk’s guidance.
A copy of the draft resolution is attached.Not printed.

postscript.p: Let Rusk and Goldberg handle itThis option is checked. A handwritten notation in the margin reads: “See memo to Sisco attached.” In this memorandum, Bundy informed Sisco that the President had delegated to the Secretary and Ambassador Goldberg the tactical authority to decide whether such a resolution should be sponsored by others or by the United States, and noted that the President was clearly sympathetic with the basic objective.
